name: Soysage
recipeIngredient: 2 pieces Kombu or kelp (about two pieces long each)
1 tbsp Canola or light sesame oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the soybeans and one piece of kombu in at least five times their volume of water overnight.
2. Discard the soaking water and kombu, rinse the beans, and cook them with the other piece of kombu in about 4°C (about 40°F) water until soft, which should take about four hours.
3. Drain the cooked beans, coarsely chop them, and mix all remaining ingredients until you have a medium stiff mixture.
4. Pack the mixture tightly into an oven-safe glass or stainless steel bowl and cover it with foil, ensuring the edges are sealed well.
5. Place a shallow, heat-resistant bowl on the bottom of a large stock pot or Dutch oven. Pour enough water into the bowl to cover it completely, then place the soysage over the hot water bath.
6. Bring the setup to a simmer and cover the pot. Steam for about an hour and a half, checking occasionally and adding more water if needed.
7. Allow the mixture to cool before using.
Makes 3 cups
